content:
First embed the picture in a convenient coordinate‐system.  Let the line \(A\) be the \(x\)–axis \(y=0\), and let circle \(B\) of radius \(R=2008\) be tangent to \(A\) at \(P=(0,0)\).  Then the center of \(B\) is \((0,R)\).  

Denote by \(r_n\) the radius of \(C_n\), and by \((x_n,r_n)\) its center (so it is tangent to \(y=0\)).  The two tangency‐to‐\(B\) and to \(y=0\) conditions give
1. \((x_n)^2+(r_n-R)^2=(r_n+R)^2\)\quad(external tangency to \(B\)),
2. the fact that \(C_n\) is tangent to \(C_{n-1}\) gives 
   \[
   (x_n-x_{n-1})^2+(r_n-r_{n-1})^2=(r_n+r_{n-1})^2.
   \]
From (1) one finds 
\[
x_n^2 \;=\;(R+r_n)^2-(R-r_n)^2
\;=\;4R\,r_n
\quad\implies\quad
x_n \;=\;2\sqrt{R\,r_n}\,.
\]
From (2) one arrives at the recurrence
\[
\sqrt{r_n}-\sqrt{r_{n-1}}
\;=\;\frac{\sqrt{r_n\,r_{n-1}}}{\sqrt R},
\]
or equivalently, setting \(s_n=\sqrt{r_n}\), 
\[
\frac1{s_{n-1}}-\frac1{s_n}
\;=\;\frac1{\sqrt R}.
\]
Since \(r_1=1\) we have \(s_1=1\), and hence
\[
\frac1{s_n}\;=\;\frac1{s_1}-\frac{n-1}{\sqrt R}
\;=\;1-\frac{n-1}{\sqrt R}
\;\Longrightarrow\;
s_n=\frac{\sqrt R}{\sqrt R-(n-1)}.
\]
Thus
\[
r_n=s_n^2
=\frac{R}{\bigl(\sqrt R-(n-1)\bigr)^2}\,.
\]
For this to make sense we need \(\sqrt R-(n-1)>0\), i.e.\ 
\[
n-1<\sqrt R\quad\Longrightarrow\quad n<\sqrt{2008}+1.
\]
Numerically \(\sqrt{2008}\approx44.81\), so the largest integer \(n\) with \(n<45.81\) is
\[
\boxed{45}.
\]
